title: Epidemiologist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run outbreak-model simulations and surveillance-data analysis end to end, but designing studies and communicating risk to policymakers stay human-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: Disease-modeling and surveillance-analytics tools with AI-assisted forecasting are mature and widely used in public health agencies.
rationales.adoption: Large public health agencies and health systems routinely use AI-assisted surveillance analytics; adoption is thinner in smaller local health departments.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run surveillance-data pipelines and flag emerging clusters, but risk communication and policy recommendation require a human epidemiologist.

tasksAtRisk: Outbreak model simulation and forecasting runs
Routine statistical report generation for case data
tasksGrowing: Study design for novel disease and exposure questions
Risk communication to policymakers and the public
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-1041, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Novel Study Design for Emerging Threats

insight: Surveillance data pipelines and forecasting models increasingly run themselves, but translating a cluster of numbers into a policy recommendation the public will trust remains an epidemiologist's job.
